DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES AND PAY SANCTIONS OF $1,080 BY DECEMBER 9, 2011. DISCOVERY REQUESTS SERVED ON A NON-HUMAN PARTY SUCH AS DEFENDANT REQUIRES THE PARTY TO DISCLOSE ALL INFORMATION THAT IS KNOWN BY THE PARTY, INCLUDING ALL OF ITS EMPLOYEES AND AGENTS, NOT JUST THE PERSON DESIGNATED TO VERIFY THE RESPONSES. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Their Requests For Admission, Set One; Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Nov-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JASON SZYDLIK, TEL. NO. 750-9900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. YASMIN GILANI OF GOINS & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (510) 663-3700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS ADOUBOU TRAORE, JOSEPH SCIARRILLO, BINIAM FANTAY AND LARRY SAXXON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Their Requests For Admission, Set One; Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions IS Granted. Defendant must provide further verified responses and pay sanctions of $1,080 by December 9, 2011. Discovery requests served on a non-human party such as defendant requires the party to disclose all information that is known by the party, including all of its employees and agents, not just the person designated to verify the responses. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Default Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar May-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: J. Willis-McGhee,
CSR: B. Fields, CSR #7235

JASON SZYDLIK, Esq. appeared for plaintiff(s) ADOUBOU TRAORE, ET AL,

Default has been entered against the Defendant(s).

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff(s), and against the Defaulted Defendant(s) THE AFRICAN IMMIGRANT & REFUGEE RESOURCE CENTER for Judgment as follows:

Damages/Principal - $29,123.06
Prejudgment Interest - $2761.78
Costs - $398.00
Total judgment in the amount of $32,282.84

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
